findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit prose instruction for `asserted_capability_confirmed` to all three verifier prompts (`code-review-verify.md`, `plan_review_verifier.md`, `plan_review_verifier_agentic.md`), mirroring the pattern used for `claims_absence`/`absence_confirmed_in_context`: specify the cohort trigger (G6/E4/T3 asserted-capability findings), the polarity (`yes`=gap confirmed, finding holds; `no`=capability present, finding refuted; `na`=default for all other findings), and the instruction to answer non-`na` only when the finding is an asserted-capability non-delivery claim.
evidence: grep for `asserted_capability_confirmed` across all files under src/rebar/llm/reviewers/ returns zero matches
code-review-verify.md lines 77–80 list `cited_reference_accurate`, `claims_absence`, `absence_confirmed_in_context` explicitly but omit `asserted_capability_confirmed`

plan_review_verifier_agentic.md lines 121–150 have dedicated paragraphs for `committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim`, `respects_artifact_altitude`, `claims_absence`, `absence_confirmed_in_context` — pattern every prior na-default field follows — but no paragraph for `asserted_capability_confirmed`

verify_models._BINARY_DESC['asserted_capability_confirmed'] contains the full rule (cohort filter + polarity) but this dict entry is a Pydantic Field(description=...) supplied as a schema hint, not injected as verifier-prompt prose
Plan text: 'Landed directly in the packaged kernel (no config flag)' — no mention of verifier-prompt update
findings.finding: The `asserted_capability_confirmed` field's processing protocol — specifically (i) that it should be answered non-`na` ONLY for findings in the G6/E4/T3 asserted-capability cohort, and (ii) the `yes`=gap-confirmed / `no`=capability-present-refutes-finding polarity — is absent from ALL three verifier prompts (`code-review-verify.md`, `plan_review_verifier.md`, `plan_review_verifier_agentic.md`). The instruction exists only in `verify_models._BINARY_DESC` as a Pydantic `Field(description=...)` value. Every comparable na-default sub-answer (`committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim`, `respects_artifact_altitude`, `claims_absence`, `absence_confirmed_in_context`) has an explicit, dedicated prose paragraph in the verifier prompts; `asserted_capability_confirmed` has none. Without a prompt-level instruction, the verifier cannot reliably know when to answer non-`na` or what the polarity means, risking silent under-application (the field stays `na` even for genuine cohort findings) or over-application outside the cohort.
scenarios: A G6/E4/T3 asserted-capability finding reaches the verifier: the model has no prompt instruction to answer non-`na`, so it defaults to `na` and the field never contributes to validity — the entire purpose of R5 is silently defeated.
A non-cohort finding reaches the verifier: without explicit instruction to default `na`, the model may answer `yes` or `no` spuriously, corrupting validity for findings outside the intended scope.
findings.suggested_fix: In each verifier prompt, after the `absence_confirmed_in_context` paragraph, add a paragraph such as: `asserted_capability_confirmed is yes|no|insufficient|na — for a finding that asserts a plan/ticket-claimed capability is NOT delivered by the code: 'yes' = the gap is confirmed (the capability is absent/unwired — the finding holds); 'no' = the capability IS present and exercised (the finding is a false non-delivery claim); 'insufficient' if the diff does not decide it. Answer 'na' — the default — for EVERY finding outside the asserted-capability cohort (criteria G6, E4, or T3). Do not answer non-na for structural, absence, or unrelated findings.

evidence: 3 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump contradiction: either update the parent epic's integration record (and re-attest the parent) to record that the bump is intentionally dropped and why, or correct the leaf AC to restore the bump — the leaf must not silently contradict a named parent deliverable.
evidence: Parent epic comment (2026-07-15): 'R5=ea39 (kernel sub-answer, gated E5+E6, IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump)'
Leaf AC bullet 4: 'IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION deliberately NOT bumped — grounded correction of the stub AC: the field is a validity axis touching neither impact_code nor impact_plan SHAPE'

Leaf plan title itself: 'R5: asserted_capability_confirmed kernel sub-answer (gated on E5+E6, version bump)' — the title retains 'version bump' while the AC retracts it, creating an internal contradiction that mirrors the parent divergence
findings.finding: The leaf (R5) redefines the IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump deliverable that the parent epic explicitly names as part of R5's scope. The parent's integration record states 'R5=ea39 (kernel sub-answer, gated E5+E6, IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump)'; the leaf's AC bullet 4 states 'IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION deliberately NOT bumped'. Under the G7 conflict rule the parent wins — the leaf cannot silently drop a named parent deliverable by substituting its own rationale.
findings.location: AC bullet 4 ('IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION deliberately NOT bumped') vs. parent epic integration-record comment ('R5=ea39 (kernel sub-answer, gated E5+E6, IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump)')
scenarios: A reviewer or implementer following the parent epic's integration record will expect a version bump as part of R5; landing without one silently violates the parent contract without a recorded parent update.

If the bump decision is genuinely reversed (as the leaf argues it should be), the parent epic's integration-record comment needs a corrective note and the parent's plan-review attestation needs re-review before R5 can be reviewed as a clean subset.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a corrective comment to the parent epic (6982-2e75-f8fa-43b9) documenting that, per E5's byte-identical requirement and the validity-axis-only nature of the change, the IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump originally noted for R5 is dropped; then update the leaf's plan title to remove 'version bump' so the title, the AC, and the parent all agree.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the 'version bump' vs 'NOT bumped' contradiction: either remove 'version bump' from the plan title (if the AC correctly states no bump) or update AC bullet 4 to reflect an actual bump, and record the single authoritative decision in one place.
evidence: Title: 'R5: asserted_capability_confirmed kernel sub-answer (gated on E5+E6, version bump)'
AC bullet 4: 'IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION deliberately NOT bumped — grounded correction of the stub AC'
findings.finding: The plan title/header ('R5: asserted_capability_confirmed kernel sub-answer (gated on E5+E6, version bump)') states 'version bump' as a deliverable, directly contradicting AC bullet 4 which says 'IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION deliberately NOT bumped'. An implementer reading the title would expect to increment a version; the AC then instructs the opposite.
scenarios: Implementer bumps IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION following the title, breaking the E5 byte-identical-sidecar property and causing downstream sidecar re-segmentation.
Reviewer flags the AC as contradicting the ticket title, requiring a re-review cycle.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove ', version bump' from the plan title so it reads 'R5: asserted_capability_confirmed kernel sub-answer (gated on E5+E6)' — the AC's rationale already explains why no bump occurs, making the title phrase redundant and wrong.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the title to remove 'version bump' (or replace with 'no version bump') so the headline faithfully reflects what AC #4 explicitly withholds — e.g. 'R5: asserted_capability_confirmed kernel sub-answer (gated on E5+E6; no IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump)'.
evidence: Title text: 'gated on E5+E6, version bump'
AC #4 text: 'IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION deliberately NOT bumped — grounded correction of the stub AC'
The Scope section lists four files and does not mention a version bump as a deliverable
findings.finding: The plan title includes 'version bump' as part of the ticket description ('gated on E5+E6, version bump'), implying a version bump is either a dependency gate or a deliverable of this unit. AC #4 explicitly and at length refutes this, stating 'IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION deliberately NOT bumped' with detailed rationale. A reader parsing only the title will expect a version bump that the body deliberately withholds, creating a headline/body contradiction.
findings.location: Title: 'R5: asserted_capability_confirmed kernel sub-answer (gated on E5+E6, version bump)' vs. AC #4: 'IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION deliberately NOT bumped'
scenarios: A reviewer or agent reading only the ticket title to triage gates will expect a version-bump deliverable and either block the ticket for missing it or apply a wrong merge criterion.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the title to: 'R5: asserted_capability_confirmed kernel sub-answer (gated on E5+E6; no IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `asserted_capability_confirmed` to the binary sub-answer instruction sections of `plan_review_verifier.md`, `plan_review_verifier_agentic.md`, and `code-review-verify.md`, including the G6/E4/T3 cohort trigger condition and the yes/no/na semantics, and enumerate these three prompt files in the plan's Scope section.
evidence: Plan Scope names only decide.py, verify_models.py, test file, and docs/review-kernel.md — verifier prompt files absent
plan_review_verifier_agentic.md lines 103–132 enumerate binary sub-questions for the LLM but omit asserted_capability_confirmed
plan_review_verifier.md lines 100–132 same omission
code-review-verify.md lines 77–84 same omission

verify_models.py _BINARY_DESC['asserted_capability_confirmed'] states verifier should answer non-na 'ONLY for findings in the asserted-capability cohort (G6/E4/T3)' — but the prompts never tell it to do so
findings.finding: The three verifier prompt files that instruct the LLM on which binary sub-answers to fill (`src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_verifier.md`, `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_verifier_agentic.md`, `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-verify.md`) are unenumerated consumers of the new `asserted_capability_confirmed` field. All three list the binary sub-questions the LLM should answer in their prompt text, but none includes `asserted_capability_confirmed`. Without a prompt instruction, the LLM will default to `na` for every finding — including G6/E4/T3 cohort findings — making the new sub-answer a permanent dead field in practice.
findings.location: Scope section; also src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_verifier_agentic.md lines 103–132, plan_review_verifier.md lines 100–132, code-review-verify.md lines 77–84
scenarios: A G6 plan-review finding about a missing asserted capability goes through Pass-2; the LLM sees no instruction for asserted_capability_confirmed and defaults to na, so the field never contributes to validity for any finding, defeating R5's stated purpose.
A code-review finding in the asserted-capability cohort similarly gets na, making the cross-gate verification counterpart inoperative.
findings.suggested_fix: In each verifier prompt's BINARY sub-answers section, add an entry such as: `- asserted_capability_confirmed — for a finding that asserts a capability the plan/ticket claimed is NOT delivered: 'yes' = gap confirmed (finding holds); 'no' = capability is present and exercised (refutes the finding); 'insufficient' if the diff does not decide it. Answer 'na' — the default — for every finding OUTSIDE the G6/E4/T3 asserted-capability cohort.`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or expand AC bullet 5) that explicitly verifies `verify.py` still exports every name that `verify_models.py` now owns, e.g. via an import-smoke test or a `__all__` assertion in `tests/unit/test_r5_asserted_capability.py`.
evidence: Scope: '(NEW module: the Pass-2 model builders were extracted from `verify.py` along the existing call-graph seam… names re-exported from `verify`, no import site changed.)'
AC bullet 5 only covers: 'module-size hard-800 respected; docs/review-kernel.md updated' — no re-export or import-site-unchanged check.
findings.finding: The Scope describes a deliverable — extracting Pass-2 model builders into a new `verify_models.py` module with re-exports from `verify.py` and no import-site changes — but no AC criterion verifies this property. An implementer could mis-export or break the re-export contract without any AC failing.
findings.location: Scope section (verify_models.py paragraph) vs. AC bullets 1–5
scenarios: verify_models.py is created but re-exports are missing or have wrong names; existing callers of verify.py break at runtime despite all ACs passing.
A follow-on refactor removes the re-exports, unaware that backward compat was a stated invariant, because no test pins it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sentence to `code-review-verify.md` and `plan_review_verifier_agentic.md` (and `plan_review_verifier.md`) explicitly instructing the verifier to answer `asserted_capability_confirmed` as `na` UNLESS the finding is in the G6/E4/T3 asserted-capability cohort, so the cohort-scoping rule is present in the human-readable verifier prompt and not only in the Pydantic field description; or, add a non-cohort na-compliance test (e.g. a parametrized unit test in `test_r5_asserted_capability.py` asserting that a mock verifier output for a non-G6/E4/T3 finding defaults the field to na from the model schema alone).
evidence: plan_review_verifier_agentic.md and code-review-verify.md contain no mention of asserted_capability_confirmed

e5_cross_gate_nonregression.py line 104: acc = 'na'  # asserted_capability_confirmed as the R5 verifier would emit it here — the harness assumes na for ALL 161 rows, never exercising a live verifier on a non-cohort finding
e6_summary.json: Exp A tests only the G6/E4/T3 cohort (50/50 findings from {G6,E4,T3}), not non-cohort na-compliance
AC3: 'validity/decision byte-identical whether the field is absent or na' — proven only for synthetic na values, not for a live verifier's non-cohort output
findings.finding: The 'conservative scope' invariant — that asserted_capability_confirmed stays na for every finding outside the G6/E4/T3 cohort — is enforced only through the Pydantic Field description in _BINARY_DESC, which is embedded in the structured-output schema but absent from all verifier prompt texts. Neither E5 nor E6 validates that a live verifier presented with a non-cohort finding actually leaves the field na: E5 constructs all 161 rows as na by design (the harness hardcodes acc='na' for every row outside the cohort), and E6 Exp A tests self-consistency only on 50 cohort (G6/E4/T3) findings. An LLM verifier that incorrectly answers 'yes' or 'no' for a non-cohort finding would silently change that finding's validity — exactly the cross-gate regression E5 aims to prevent but structurally cannot catch.
findings.location: Plan: 'Scope' section + AC3 ('Code-review behaviour pinned UNCHANGED'); verify_models.py:_BINARY_DESC asserted_capability_confirmed entry; code-review-verify.md (no mention of field); e5_cross_gate_nonregression.py:line 104 (acc = 'na' hardcoded)
scenarios: A code-review verifier, seeing a non-G6/E4/T3 finding about (e.g.) a dead-code defect, reads the field description and — because it is described in detail — answers 'no' (not applicable rather than na), inadvertently lowering that finding's validity and changing its decision from advisory to dropped.

A plan-review verifier batch includes both an asserted-capability finding (G6) and a non-cohort finding (E1) in the same chunk; the verifier fills asserted_capability_confirmed for the E1 finding by mistake, non-idempotently changing a historically-stable advisory to dropped.
findings.suggested_fix: In each verifier prompt, add to the binary sub-answers section: `asserted_capability_confirmed is yes|no|insufficient|na — ONLY for a finding asserting that a plan/ticket-claimed capability is NOT delivered: yes = gap confirmed (finding holds); no = capability is present (finding refuted, validity drops); insufficient = diff does not decide. Answer na — the default — for EVERY finding that is NOT about an asserted but undelivered capability (i.e., every finding outside the G6/E4/T3 cohort).` This makes the cohort rule present in the prompt, not only in the schema.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete jq cardinality-assertion command to AC1 (e.g., `jq '[.[] | select(.decision_diff != null or .priority_diff != null or .validity_diff != null)] | length' docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e5_nonregression_diff.json` must equal 0) so the empty-diff property is machine-verifiable.

No jq command or CI assertion is specified to confirm the JSON contains zero non-null diff entries.
Matches VERIFY-COMMAND DEFECT TAXONOMY items (5) missing cardinality assertion and (6) prose-vs-structured assertion target.
findings.finding: AC1 asserts a cardinality claim ('empty decision/priority/validity diff') against a named JSON artifact (`e5_nonregression_diff.json`) but provides no concrete jq assertion to verify the claim; a reviewer or CI step cannot distinguish a genuinely empty diff from one that was never generated or was silently truncated.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'empty decision/priority/validity diff over the 161-row code-review corpus'
scenarios: An executor commits a partial or malformed artifact; the AC reads as satisfied because the file exists, but diffs are actually present.
CI or a future reviewer cannot reproducibly check the artifact without a specified command.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC1: `Verified by: jq -e '[.[] | select(.decision_diff != null or .priority_diff != null or .validity_diff != null)] | length == 0' docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e5_nonregression_diff.json`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit proving commands for the two negative constraints in AC4 and AC5, e.g., `grep -c 'IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION' src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` unchanged between base and HEAD for AC4, and `wc -l src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify_models.py | awk '$1 <= 800'` for AC5.

'module-size hard-800 respected'
No grep, wc -l, or git diff command is given to verify either negative constraint.
findings.finding: AC4 and AC5 make 'NOT done' and size-boundary claims ('IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION deliberately NOT bumped', 'module-size hard-800 respected') with no proving commands; an executor has no specified check to confirm either constraint is satisfied after the change.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 ('IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION deliberately NOT bumped') and AC5 ('module-size hard-800 respected')
scenarios: A version constant is accidentally bumped in an unrelated edit; no AC command catches it.
verify_models.py grows past 800 LOC during the extraction; there is no CI gate or in-plan wc check to enforce the cap.
findings.suggested_fix: AC4: `git diff origin/main HEAD -- src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py | grep '+IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION' | wc -l` must equal 0. AC5: `wc -l src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify_models.py | awk '{exit ($1 > 800)}'`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the Scope section whether `verify_models.py` extraction is a prerequisite (done in a prior ticket) or in-scope for this ticket, and add an AC (or reference an existing one) that verifies the re-export resolves correctly (e.g., `python -c 'from rebar.llm.review_kernel.verify import _build_binary'` exits 0).
evidence: '(NEW module: the Pass-2 model builders were extracted from verify.py along the existing call-graph seam to hold the LOCKED 800-LOC hard cap — verify.py sat at exactly 800; names re-exported from verify, no import site changed.)'
The past tense 'were extracted' conflicts with 'NEW module' in the same parenthetical.
No AC verifies that `from rebar.llm.review_kernel.verify import _build_binary` (or equivalent re-export) still resolves correctly post-extraction.
findings.finding: The Scope section introduces `verify_models.py` with ambiguous tense ('the Pass-2 model builders were extracted from `verify.py`') — past tense suggests the extraction is pre-existing, but the file appears in the Scope bullet as work to be done in this ticket. An executor cannot determine whether to perform the module extraction or assume it is already complete, and no AC covers re-export correctness.
findings.location: Scope section — '`src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify_models.py` — `_BINARY_DESC` + `_BINARY_NA_DEFAULT` entry. (NEW module: the Pass-2 model builders were extracted from `verify.py` …)'
scenarios: Executor assumes extraction is pre-done and only adds the new field, leaving verify.py over 800 LOC because the extraction was never performed.
Executor performs the extraction in this ticket but breaks an import site that was not tested, since no AC covers the re-export.
findings.suggested_fix: If the extraction is prior work: change 'NEW module' to 'pre-existing module (extracted in R4)' and remove it from this ticket's scope bullets. If it is in-scope: add AC: `python -c 'from rebar.llm.review_kernel.verify import _build_binary; assert callable(_build_binary)'` exits 0.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out plan (e.g., 'revert the single GRADED_BINARY entry and the verify_models.py _BINARY_NA_DEFAULT entry; re-run E5 replay to confirm restoration') in the plan or in `docs/review-kernel.md`.
evidence: Why: 'the kernel vocabulary is SHARED with code-review… this is the epic's highest-blast-radius change'
AC bullet 1 describes E5 as a pre-landing gate artifact, not a rollback path.
No section of the plan describes a back-out step, a feature flag, or an expand-contract revert sequence.
findings.finding: The plan modifies a shared kernel used by both plan-review and code-review pipelines but provides no explicit rollback or back-out plan. The E5 gate proves pre-landing byte-equivalence but is a verification artifact, not a recovery mechanism; if a post-landing defect is found, there is no described procedure to revert.
findings.location: Why section ('highest-blast-radius change'); AC bullets 1, 3; Scope
scenarios: Post-landing, a subtle validity mis-scoring is discovered on a non-cohort row; the team needs to revert but has no documented procedure.
The Gerrit +1/Verified gate passes, code lands, and a production incident requires emergency rollback with no recorded steps.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief rejection note (e.g., a callout or inline comment) to `docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md:197` recording that the `divergent_implementation` coupling was evaluated and deliberately NOT adopted in R5 (with a pointer to `docs/review-kernel.md`), so the decision is traceable from both documents.
evidence: 'The research-doc coupling is explicitly NOT replicated; the sub-answer's only effect is through validity(). Recorded in docs/review-kernel.md.'
No mention of annotating or updating docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md to reflect the rejection decision.
findings.finding: The plan explicitly reconciles against `docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md:197` (the divergent_implementation coupling), recording the rejection in `docs/review-kernel.md` — but does NOT update the research doc itself. A future developer reading that doc will see a recommended coupling with no record that it was deliberately rejected for R5, risking re-introduction.
scenarios: A future developer reads task-decomposition-sota-2026.md and implements the divergent_implementation coupling, re-introducing the cross-field coupling R5 explicitly rejected.
A reviewer of a future ticket sees the research doc's recommendation and treats it as still-open guidance.
findings.suggested_fix: In `docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md` near line 197, add: `> **Decision (R5):** The divergent_implementation coupling described here was evaluated and deliberately not adopted. See docs/review-kernel.md §asserted_capability_confirmed for rationale.`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the 800-LOC hard cap and the re-export extraction pattern as an architectural constraint in `docs/review-kernel.md` (or AGENTS.md) so it is discoverable by future contributors adding to the kernel.
evidence: 'NEW module: the Pass-2 model builders were extracted from verify.py along the existing call-graph seam to hold the LOCKED 800-LOC hard cap — verify.py sat at exactly 800; names re-exported from verify, no import site changed.'
docs/review-kernel.md is listed for update but the 800-LOC cap as an architectural rule is not mentioned as being documented there.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new module `verify_models.py` extracted from `verify.py` under the 800-LOC hard cap architectural constraint, but neither an ADR nor AGENTS.md update is mentioned for the module-size cap pattern itself — only `review-kernel.md` is listed for update. Future contributors hitting the cap in other modules have no canonical doc to find this constraint.
scenarios: A contributor grows another kernel module past 800 LOC, unaware of the cap, and the cap is enforced only at review time rather than being a discoverable documented constraint.
A contributor sees the re-export pattern in verify.py without knowing why it exists and collapses the extraction in a refactor.
findings.suggested_fix: In `docs/review-kernel.md`, add a 'Module size constraint' section: 'Kernel modules are capped at 800 LOC. When a module reaches the cap, extract cohesive sub-modules and re-export from the original to preserve import stability (see verify_models.py extracted from verify.py in R5).'

The plan treats this as a pre-landing requirement, yet no harness, generation command, or corpus definition for the 'E5 byte-identical replay over the 161-row code-review corpus' is specified — neither the corpus source nor the replay driver is named.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1: 'Gate-row evidence committed before landing: E5 byte-identical replay artifact (`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e5_nonregression_diff.json` — empty decision/priority/validity diff over the 161-row code-review corpus, `na` outside G6/E4/T3`)'

findings.finding: The E5 byte-identical replay artifact (`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e5_nonregression_diff.json`) referenced in AC1 does not exist in the repository, and no harness, methodology, or driver for producing it is described in the plan's Scope section. The plan states this artifact must be 'committed before landing' but does not specify which code produces it, what corpus it replays, or how 'byte-identical' is verified — leaving the gate proof method unverified.
findings.location: AC1: 'E5 byte-identical replay artifact (docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e5_nonregression_diff.json — empty decision/priority/validity diff over the 161-row code-review corpus, na outside G6/E4/T3)'

findings.finding: The plan (Why section, AC1) asserts E6 Exp A gates whether 'R5's new Pass-2 sub-answer (asserted_capability_confirmed) is worth adding' and records 'κ=0.874 PASS' as evidence the sub-answer emits stably. But e6_summary.json confirms agreement_unit='pass3 decision in {block,advisory,dropped,indeterminate}' — E6 measured consistency of the *existing* decision field, not the new sub-answer. The README explicitly states 'asserted_capability_confirmed does not exist yet — E6 records only the *existing* decision, never any R5 field.' E6 is a judge-stability prerequisite, not a direct self-consistency gate for the new sub-answer itself.
findings.location: AC1 ('E6 Exp A κ≥0.6 (recorded κ=0.874 PASS)') and Why section ('sits behind the strictest gates (E5 byte-identical replay + E6 self-consistency κ=0.874)')
findings.suggested_fix: In AC1, change 'E6 Exp A κ≥0.6 (recorded κ=0.874 PASS)' to read: 'E6 Exp A κ≥0.6 (recorded κ=0.874 PASS on the existing `decision` field — judge-stability prerequisite). NOTE: direct stability of `asserted_capability_confirmed` is post-landing measurable only; E6 establishes the judge is stable enough to build on, not that this specific sub-answer is stable.'

findings.finding: The plan presents E6's kappa=0.874 as the gate that validates R5's new sub-answer ('asserted_capability_confirmed') emits stably enough to be worth adding. But the E6 README explicitly states the sub-answer 'does NOT exist yet — E6 records only the existing decision, never any R5 field.' E6 measures pre-R5 Pass-2/Pass-3 judge self-consistency on decision outcomes, not the new field's emission stability. The only measured signal for the sub-answer itself is asserted_capability_sanity.json (N=6, single-run, kappa=0.667 on the finding-fire dimension, not on sub-answer values). The plan's stated gate signal ('self_consistency.pass → whether R5's new Pass-2 sub-answer emits stably enough to be worth adding') is unverified by E6 as conducted.
findings.location: AC1 (gate-row evidence) and 'Why' / 'What' sections; docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/README.md:186-187; e6_prereg.json gate_signal
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence in AC1 or the 'Why' section clarifying: 'E6 kappa=0.874 validates the underlying judge's decision stability; the new sub-answer's emission stability is bounded by asserted_capability_sanity.json (N=6, kappa=0.667 on fire/no-fire) and is fail-safe by design (na-default means a non-emitting verifier abstains rather than dragging validity).'. This resolves the capability-assertion gap without requiring a new experiment.

findings.finding: The leaf redefines R5's core deliverable in two ways that contradict the parent's plan without updating the parent first: (1) the parent's integration record explicitly names 'IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump' as part of R5's scope, but the leaf's AC4 drops the bump calling it a 'stub AC'; (2) the authoritative research spec the parent implements (docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md:197) requires that a `no` answer 'feeds the existing impact axes (divergent_implementation), lifting priority', but the leaf routes the sub-answer to the validity axis and explicitly forbids the divergent_implementation coupling. Both changes step outside the parent's declared scope without a prior parent update.
findings.location: Leaf: AC4 ('IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION deliberately NOT bumped') and 'Design reconciliation' section ('No cross-field coupling'); Parent: integration comment 'R5=ea39 (kernel sub-answer, gated E5+E6, IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump)' and docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md:197
findings.suggested_fix: Add a comment to the parent epic (6982) explicitly recording the design decision: that R5's `asserted_capability_confirmed` is placed on the validity axis (not impact), that the `divergent_implementation` coupling described in sota-2026.md:197 is deliberately NOT implemented, and that the IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump originally attributed to R5 is dropped with rationale. This realigns the parent's plan to the leaf's actual scope so the leaf is a consistent narrowing of a corrected parent rather than a silent divergence.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
